pr.title: Convert Special attributes in Fighter struct to a union.
pr.author: RevoSucks

summary: Converted the Fighter struct's shared special-attribute region around 0x222C-0x2340 from flat anonymous fields into a per-character union named sa, then updated known character code to access those fields through fighter-specific views such as ft->sa.mario, ft->sa.clink, ft->sa.mars, ft->sa.fox, ft->sa.ness, and ft->sa.peach. The PR author noted that access patterns suggested this region should be a union and that converting known accesses still matched the DOL.
what_changed: In src/melee/ft/fighter.h, replaced flat Fighter fields x222C through x224C plus filler_x224C with a union member named sa covering the same special-attribute region before x2340_stateVar1.
Added many SpecialAttrs_* structs in fighter.h, each sized to the apparent 0x222C-0x2340 region using explicit u32 fields and filler arrays.

Updated character code to access special attributes through the new union, for example ft->x222C became ft->sa.mars.x222C in Marth/Roy code, ft->sa.fox.x222C in Fox/Falco code, ft->sa.mario.x2240 in Mario/Dr. Mario projectile handling, and ft->sa.clink.x2244 in Young Link code.
Documented clone sharing inside the union with comments: Mario SpecialAttrs is used for DrMario, Fox for Falco, Captain for Ganondorf, and Mars/Marth for Emblem/Roy.

Preserved matching while making field ownership more explicit; the PR body says the author determined the 0x222C to 0x2340 range was favorable to union accesses and that the DOL still matched after converting known accesses.
smart_moves: Used a union instead of duplicating or renaming global Fighter fields, reflecting that the same memory range has different meanings depending on the fighter.
Kept field offsets visible in comments such as /* 0x222C */ and maintained filler sizes so layout-sensitive decompilation remains auditable.
Converted call sites mechanically to the appropriate fighter-specific union arm, reducing ambiguity without changing behavior.
Handled clone characters by intentionally reusing the base character's special-attribute struct where the diff evidence showed equivalent layout usage.
Left TODO comments acknowledging that the new definitions may eventually belong in per-fighter headers, avoiding overclaiming final organization.
decomp_lessons: When a shared actor or fighter struct has a region whose fields are interpreted differently by different subclasses, a union can improve type accuracy while preserving binary layout.
For matching-sensitive struct work, keep offsets and filler arrays explicit so later contributors can verify that replacing flat fields did not move following members.
Convert all known accesses in the same PR when introducing a unionized region; partial conversion would leave mixed field styles and make future matching/debugging harder.
Clone fighters may legitimately share special-attribute structs when evidence shows they use the same layout, but this should be documented at the union site.
A broad header refactor can still be a matching tactic if the access form is compatible with generated code; the author explicitly tested that the DOL still matched.
naming_conventions: Per-fighter special attribute structs were named SpecialAttrs_<Character>, for example SpecialAttrs_Mario, SpecialAttrs_Fox, SpecialAttrs_CLink, SpecialAttrs_Mars, and SpecialAttrs_Mewtwo.
The Fighter union member was named sa, producing access patterns like ft->sa.mario.x222C and ft->sa.clink.x2244.
Union arms used lowercase fighter names such as mario, fox, captain, koopa, ness, peach, mewtwo, mars, clink, masterhand, crazyhand, sandbag.
Unknown fields retained offset-style names such as x222C, x2230, x2240, and x2244 rather than inventing semantic names.
The PR used the existing project spelling Mars for Marth-related structs and comments, and Seak for Sheik-like naming, as shown in fighter.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR body states that the author tested the union conversion and the DOL still matched.
The converted region was selected based on access patterns and a favorable range from 0x222C to 0x2340, possibly up to that boundary.
The struct definitions use fillers sized to maintain the exact layout through the special-attribute region, ending before Fighter::x2340_stateVar1.
Access rewrites preserved the same underlying offsets while changing C syntax from ft->xNNNN to ft->sa.<fighter>.xNNNN.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only explicit rationale in the slice is from the PR body: the members probably need to be accessed via a union based on how they are accessed, and the DOL still matched after conversion.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that moved SpecialAttrs_* definitions out of fighter.h into per-fighter headers.
Search for remaining direct Fighter accesses to x222C, x2230, x2234, x2238, x223C, x2240, x2244, x2248, or x224C after PR 342.
Look for later semantic renames of fields inside SpecialAttrs_Mario, SpecialAttrs_CLink, SpecialAttrs_Ness, SpecialAttrs_Peach, and SpecialAttrs_Mars.
Investigate whether the special-attribute union range was later extended beyond 0x2340 or refined with smaller per-fighter structs.
